codeReview.summary: The commit moves provenance in the right direction, but it still does not provide true request-scoped safety on the live shared MCP server, leaves service-generated CreateWork provenance incomplete in the feature diff, and never exercises the production MCP transport end-to-end.

issues.description: CreatedBy(ctx) still falls back to mutable server state, and the shared server is flipped to supervisor mode via SetCallerRole("supervisor") in internal/cli/supervisor_agent.go:107 while the same mcpServer instance is also mounted at /mcp in internal/cli/serve.go:281. WithCallerRole exists only in tests, so concurrent external MCP traffic can still inherit supervisor provenance.

The feature changes ActorFromCreatedBy's default behavior, but the feature diff does not update the existing service-generated CreateWork/bootstrap/attestation callsites that omit CreatedBy (see the prior failure at service.go:3908, service.go:6000, and bootstrap.go:204). Those follow-on paths still regress ActorService provenance until the callsites explicitly pass CreatedBy="service".

The new regression coverage remains helper-only: it asserts CreatedBy()/ActorFromCreatedBy directly and never drives the production MCP mutation path through session.CallTool or the StreamableHTTP handler, so the requested live-handler end-to-end proof is still missing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: .factory/library/contracts.md still frames the provenance gap as a generic session-context problem, but it does not capture the follow-up requirement to explicitly tag true service-generated work creation paths with CreatedBy="service". That omission makes the service-provenance regression easy to rediscover instead of avoiding it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/contracts.md:27-28; internal/service/service.go prior review lines 3908/6000 and internal/service/bootstrap.go:204

sharedStateObservations.observation: The control-plane-worker procedure asks for provenance verification across CLI, HTTP, MCP, and service-generated follow-on paths, but the transcript skeleton shows the worker stayed at helper-level assertions and build checks instead of exercising the live MCP transport path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: internal/mcpserver/server_provenance_test.go:185-210; transcript skeleton in worker-transcripts.jsonl shows no session.CallTool or serve/HTTP validation steps

summary: FAIL: the fix still relies on mutable shared MCP provenance state, leaves service-generated provenance incomplete in the feature diff, and lacks live-handler end-to-end coverage.
